predicted_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The client has had past experiences where they felt their feelings didn't matter, leading to a reluctance to share and a desire to protect themselves from disappointment.
predicted_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: People only reach out when they need something and don't genuinely care.
predicted_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs: If I share too much, it might not matter; it's safer to keep things to myself to avoid getting hurt.
predicted_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: Start with lighter topics in conversations, remind myself it's okay to take my time, and have written reminders to refer to when feeling anxious.

ground_truth_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The client has had experiences of feeling unheard and emotionally invalidated since a young age, such as the recollection of being chastised for crying as a child. They have struggled with depression. Their interactions with Edson and others may have reactivated these historical feelings of invalidation.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: I am unlovable. | I am undesirable, unwanted. | I am bound to be rejected. | I am bound to be abandoned. | I am bound to be alone.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs:
People will not find me worth their time. If someone does not reach out to me, it means they do not value me. If I am not explicitly included, I am unwanted. My worth is contingent on others' acceptance.
Depressive variant: When I am depressed, no one will want to be around me. If I'm not perfect, people will reject me. My value to friends is conditional on my ability to be socially available and upbeat.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The client uses avoidance like declining invitations or rebuffing attempts to interact (such as Edson's dinner invitation) as a protective measure. They seek affirmation from others to combat feelings of rejection but also distance themselves from situations where they could potentially feel hurt.
